utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , louis . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi . good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's good to see you as well . are you ready to get started ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: louis is a 58-year-old male here for follow up from an emergency room visit . so , louis , what happened ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i was playing tennis on saturday . it was really , really hot that day , very humid . and about after about a half an hour i was very short of breath , i was struggling breathing . i thought i was having a heart attack , got really nervous . so , my wife took me to the er and , uh , everything checked out , but i was just very upset about it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and how have you been feeling since that time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , foof , probably , probably about six hours after we got home , i felt very light-head and very dizzy and then , sunday , i felt fine . i just thought it was worth checking up with you though .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and have you been taking all of your meds for your heart failure ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have . i have . i've been , uh , very diligent with it . and , uh , i'm in touch with the doctor and so far , so good , other than this episode on saturday .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , and you're watching your diet , you're avoiding salt . have you had anything salty ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i cheat every now and then . you know , i try and stay away from the junk food and the salty foods . but , for the most part , i've been doing a good job of that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , and i know that they removed a cataract from your eye-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . a couple of , like couple months ago . that's been fine ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that was three months ago , thursday , and everything's been fine ever since .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , no vision problems .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you had a skin cancer removed about five months ago as well . you've had a lot going on .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . it's been a really busy year . an- and again , so far , so good . that healed up nicely , no problems ever since .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , so , why do n't we go ahead and we'll do a quick physical-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . exam . hey , dragon , show me the blood pressure . so , here , your blood pressure is a little high .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so , you know , i did see a report in the emergency room that your blood pressure was high there as well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , we'll have to just kind of talk about that . uh , but let's go ahead and we'll examine you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , you know , looking at you , your neck is very supple . i do n't appreciate any fibular venous distention . your heart is a regular rate and rhythm , no murmur . your lungs have some fine crackles in them , bilaterally . and you have trace lower extremity edema in both legs . so , what that means , essentially , is that you may have some extra fluid on board , um , from eating salty foods-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . along with this history of your congestive heart failure . but , let's go ahead and look at some of your results . hey , dragon , show me the ecg . so , this is , uh , a s- a stable ecg for you . this basically shows that you have some left ventricular hypertrophy which caused your congestive heart failure . um , let's go ahead and review your echocardiogram . hey , dragon , show me the echocardiogram . so , in reviewing the results of your echocardiogram , it shows that your pumping function of your heart is a little low , uh , but it's stable . and , you know , i think that we know this and we have you on the appropriate-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: medication therapy . and then , i just wan na be reminded about , um , the results of your skin biopsy . hey , dragon , show me the skin biopsy results . okay . and in reviewing the pathology report for your skin cancer-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . you know , it looks like they got all of that and everything's fine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , you know , my impression of you at this time , for the shortness of breath that you had in the emergency department , i think it was an exacerbation of your heart failure . and you probably had some , what we call , dietary indiscretion , you ate some salty food which made you retain some fluid .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , for that , i'm going to prescribe you , you know , an extra dose of lasix 80 milligrams , once a day . and , um , we're going to , uh , put you on some carvedilol 25 milligrams , twice a day . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . perfect .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and i think from a , a cataract surgery standpoint and your skin cancer removal , everything seems to be fine and you're doing well , so i do n't think we need to adjust any of those medications .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good to hear .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ? hey , dragon , order lasix 80 milligrams , once a day . hey , dragon , order carvedilol 25 milligrams , twice a day . okay . and the nurse will come in and she'll see you soon . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: Louis Williams is a 58-year-old male presenting for follow-up after an emergency room visit.
Congestive heart failure
The patient experienced shortness of breath and dyspnea while playing tennis in hot weather.
The doctor asks how the patient has been feeling since the emergency room visit.
The patient reports lightheadedness and dizziness following the emergency room visit, but felt fine by Sunday.
The doctor asks if the patient has been taking his medications for congestive heart failure.
The patient reports being diligent with his heart failure medications.
The doctor asks if the patient has been consuming salty foods.
The patient admits to occasional dietary indiscretion regarding salt intake.
Cataract surgery
The doctor asks if the patient has had any issues since his cataract surgery.
The patient had cataract extraction three months ago and reports no issues.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing any vision problems.
The patient denies any current vision problems.
Skin cancer
The patient had skin cancer removed five months ago and reports the site is well-healed.
Physical exam findings include supple neck, no jugular venous distention, regular heart rate and rhythm, no murmurs, fine crackles in lungs, and bilateral lower extremity edema.
ECG shows left ventricular hypertrophy and echocardiogram shows stable diminished ejection fraction.
Skin cancer pathology report confirms clear margins.
The doctor attributes the emergency room visit to an exacerbation of heart failure due to dietary indiscretion.
The doctor prescribes Lasix 80 mg once a day and carvedilol 25 mg twice a day.
The doctor determines no treatment changes are needed for the patient's cataract or skin cancer history.

case_description: ER follow-up; CHF;hx cataract and skin cancer
